summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates npm credentials, environment, and filesystem contents to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that reads /root/.npmrc, /app/.npmrc, /usr/etc/npmrc, the full sorted process environment, /app source files, /flag* files, recursive scans for files matching flag/secret/HTB patterns, and /proc/*/cmdline. The collected output is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201 over plaintext HTTP. Execution is gated by isChallengeWorker(): the payload only fires when cwd contains /app/node_modules, the hostname matches /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/ (Docker container default), and the npm registry is not a Chinese mirror — an evasion fingerprint designed to hide from developer laptops and scanners while firing on the intended victim host class. Any host that matches these conditions on install will leak its npm auth tokens (enabling attacker republication under the victim's identity), CI secrets in env vars, and arbitrary readable filesystem contents to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
